title: Training Philosophy
description: A source's non-negotiable guiding principles. Governs what the system will and won't do when drawing from this methodology.

id.description: Unique slug. Used as reference key across the system. e.g. 'starting_strength', 'uphill_athlete'

name.description: Human-readable name. e.g. 'Starting Strength / Mark Rippetoe'

core_principles.description: Non-negotiable beliefs expressed as short slug strings. These are the axioms — the system won't violate them when this source is active.

scope.description: Which modalities this philosophy claims authority over. The system only applies this source's rules in sessions belonging to these modalities.

bias.description: Modalities this philosophy actively emphasizes. Subset of scope.

avoid_with.description: Modality IDs this philosophy conflicts with. Expresses which training qualities should not be combined with this methodology at high volume. Use modality IDs (e.g. 'max_strength', 'aerobic_base') — not philosophy names.

required_equipment.description: Equipment that must be present for this philosophy to be applicable. If missing, system cannot draw from this source.

intensity_model.description: How this philosophy approaches intensity across the week.

progression_philosophy.description: Primary mechanism by which this source defines progress.

sources.description: Source documents or URLs this entry was derived from.

notes.description: Free-text notes, known gaps, or caveats about this entry.
